Final road book · two mates · one grand-tourer

Highlands
Night Run

East London → Moffat → Glencoe & Glen Etive → Kinlochleven → Edinburgh → planned sleep at Wetherby → East London on Saturday morning.

Wed 22–Sat 25 July 2026 08:00–08:30 rental pickup Approx. 1,000–1,100 miles Photography + scenic roads Kinlochleven confirmed choice
At a glance Wednesday Thursday Friday Night rest Rental car Checklist
Locked itinerary

Choose Kinlochleven over Fort William.

Kinlochleven is the better fit for this particular trip: the Thursday finish feels properly Highland, the B863 around Loch Leven is an enjoyable final driving leg, and Friday still connects cleanly to Edinburgh through Glencoe, Crianlarich and Callander.

Book the available Kinlochleven room now. Keep Fort William only as the fallback if the booking disappears or has unsuitable parking/check-in terms.
Wed · MoffatMostly cloudy, chance of a shower
23° / 13°C
Thu · KinlochlevenCloudy with some light rain
18° / 13°C
Fri · EdinburghBreezy, clouds and sunny spells
20° / 13°C

Forecast snapshot checked Tuesday 21 July. Re-check before every long leg.

Route logic

One clean arc

WED
East London → Moffat

The northbound warm-up

Target: collect at 08:00–08:30, complete paperwork and leave by about 09:00. Use M11 → A14 → M6; take the M6 Toll only if live traffic makes it worthwhile.

08:00–09:00

Collect and inspect the car

Photograph every wheel, panel and windscreen. Confirm both drivers are on the rental agreement, tyre-inflation kit/spare arrangements, fuel policy and Saturday return procedure.

11:00

First 15–20 minute break

Keep it functional. Swap drivers even if the first driver feels fine.

14:00–14:45

Tebay Services

Lunch, fuel check and a proper walk. Do not turn this into a long sit-down stop if traffic north is building.

17:00–18:30

Devil’s Beef Tub — energy permitting

Use the designated A701 lay-by. Skip it without regret if arrival has slipped or the weather is poor.

Evening

Moffat: park, eat, stop driving

Once the car is parked for the night, this is your best opportunity for a relaxed whisky with dinner. Keep it modest and allow a full night’s sleep before Thursday.

Open Wednesday route Moffat accommodation map
THU
Moffat → Glen Etive → Kinlochleven

The full cinematic day

Leave: 07:00–07:30. Route north via M74, Glasgow’s western side and the A82 along Loch Lomond. Allow the whole day; Highland mileage is slow mileage.

Morning

Loch Lomond and Crianlarich

Use a short shoreline stop only if parking is easy. Take breakfast/coffee around Tarbet, Crianlarich or Tyndrum.

Late morning

Rannoch Moor

Photo stops only in proper lay-bys. The A82 currently has temporary lights around Kingshouse/Loch Ba, so carry timing slack.

Midday–afternoon

Glen Etive out-and-back

Turn onto the single-track road at the signed junction near Buachaille Etive Mòr. The complete run to Loch Etive and back can consume two hours with photos. Turn around early if rain, traffic or fatigue builds.

Afternoon

Glencoe photo sequence

Buachaille viewpoint → Three Sisters → Glencoe village. Keep stops short and avoid overfilling the day.

17:30–19:00

B863 to Kinlochleven

Finish around Loch Leven and check in. This road is scenic but can be narrow and slow; never park in passing places.

Weather rule: Thursday is forecast cloudy with light rain. Moody skies are excellent for photography, but abandon Glen Etive’s full out-and-back if water, visibility or concentration becomes an issue.
Open Thursday route Traffic Scotland live incidents
FRI
Kinlochleven → Edinburgh → Wetherby

Highlands to city, then the controlled night run

07:00–07:30

Leave Kinlochleven

Take B863 to Glencoe, then A82 south → Crianlarich → A85/A84 through Lochearnhead and Callander → M9 toward Edinburgh. Allow roughly 3½ hours including a coffee stop.

10:30–11:30

Park at Hermiston Park & Ride

Hermiston is a 24-hour site and permits overnight parking for up to three nights, which removes the risk of being trapped by a late return. Use live public-transport directions into the centre.

11:30–17:15

Compact Edinburgh walk

Princes Street → Old Town/Victoria Street → The Vennel → Royal Mile → Calton Hill if time and legs allow. Eat an early dinner before returning to the car.

Whisky interest

Browse, buy — do not taste

Because the overnight drive follows, treat Edinburgh’s whisky venues as shops and displays only. No alcohol for either potential driver.

18:00–18:30

Depart Edinburgh

Primary route: A720 → A1/A1(M) south. Stop near Washington for 15–20 minutes, then continue to Wetherby. Check direction-specific overnight works before committing to the A1.

22:00–23:00

Wetherby Services: stop for the night

Do not continue toward London. Pay the 2–24 hour parking charge immediately and begin the planned rest period.

Closure fallback: if National Highways shows a disruptive southbound A1/A1(M) closure, use A720 → M8 → M74 → M6 → A14 → M11 and choose a paid motorway-services rest stop on that route instead.
Open route to Edinburgh Open night leg Check English road closures
22:30–04:30 target

Wetherby rest plan

  • First two hours are free; current Moto price is £15 for 2–24 hours.
  • Pay immediately and retain the confirmation.
  • Engine off, doors locked, slight ventilation.
  • Use eye masks, travel pillows and light blankets.
  • Set two alarms and allow at least 5–6 hours stopped.
  • Do not drive again if either driver wakes groggy or unwell.
The car is a fallback bed, not a hotel. Two adults will be uncomfortable. The purpose is sufficient rest, not arriving at a particular minute.
Saturday morning

Wetherby → East London

Target departure: 04:15–05:00 only after genuine sleep.

Continue A1(M)/A1, then follow live navigation toward M11 and East London. Include one 15-minute break around the halfway point and swap drivers.

ScenarioLikely arrival
Depart around 04:30, normal trafficAbout 08:30–09:30
Late departure or closure diversion10:00 or later
Either driver still tiredRest longer; arrival time becomes irrelevant
Open Saturday route

The right mid-range car

Best category

Large estate or comfortable medium SUV

Top choices: Škoda Superb Estate, Volvo V60/XC60, BMW 3 or 5 Series Touring, Audi A4/A6 Avant, Mercedes E-Class Estate or equivalent.

An estate is better than a sporty saloon because the luggage stays organised and the one-night emergency rest is less punishing.

Rental essentials

Book the category, inspect the actual car

  • Automatic gearbox and adaptive cruise if available.
  • Both drivers formally added.
  • Avoid very large SUVs on Glen Etive/B863.
  • Avoid low sports suspension and very thin tyres.
  • Confirm unlimited or sufficient mileage.
  • Confirm the Saturday return window and key-drop rules.
Planning numbers

Time, distance and fuel

LegPlanning estimateNotes
East London → Moffat7–8½ hours driving + stopsRental collection and London traffic can move the entire day.
Moffat → Kinlochleven4–5 hours direct; full day with stopsGlen Etive adds up to two hours.
Kinlochleven → EdinburghAbout 3–3½ hoursAllow more for temporary signals and photography.
Edinburgh → WetherbyAbout 3½–4 hoursIncludes one proper break.
Wetherby → East LondonAbout 4 hours + breakEarly-Saturday traffic should be lighter, but closures dominate.
FuelRoughly £170–£230Assumes 1,000–1,100 miles, 35–45 mpg and a planning fuel price—not a quote.

Book tonight

  • Moffat room with parking and late check-in.
  • The available Kinlochleven room with parking.
  • Large estate/premium touring category with two drivers.
  • Download offline maps for Scotland and the A1 corridor.
  • Save rental breakdown and roadside-assistance numbers.

Pack in one small bag

  • Waterproof shells and dry socks.
  • Travel pillows, eye masks and two light blankets.
  • Car charger, power bank and offline music.
  • Lens cloths, rain cover and spare camera battery.
  • Water and simple food for the Wetherby stop.
  • No valuables visible while parked in Edinburgh.

Road rules that matter here

Single track

Use passing places to pass—not park. Let following traffic overtake and acknowledge considerate drivers.

Fatigue

Plan a break at least every two hours and avoid trying to “make up time” after midnight.

Photography

Only stop where the entire vehicle is safely off the carriageway. A missed photograph is cheaper than a collision.

Live planning sources

  • Traffic Scotland roadworks — A82 restrictions and temporary lights.
  • National Highways road-closure report — check A1/A1(M) on Friday.
  • Highway Code, rules 89–102 — fatigue and break guidance.
  • Hermiston Park & Ride — 24-hour operation and overnight parking allowance.
  • Moto Wetherby — 24/7 services and parking price.

All driving times are planning estimates. Live traffic, roadworks, rental delays, weather and rest needs take priority. The embedded photographs make this file self-contained and usable offline.

Highlands Night Run · Final Road Book
Moffat first. Kinlochleven for the Highland night. Edinburgh for the finale. Wetherby for real rest—not bravado.
